_doc: Real gate-failure cases from the peptides campaign, with WRONG (what the ranker chose) vs CORRECT (where the point actually starts/resolves) boundaries. Correct boundaries were determined by a human reading the raw transcript (docs/CLIP_QUALITY_FIX_SPEC.md PART B3). These drive (1) contrasting-pair few-shots in the segmentation/ranking + verifier prompts and (2) regression cases in the eval harness (make eval-segmentation). Timestamps are seconds; correct_start/correct_end must sit on sentence boundaries in the source transcript.

pairs.wrong_reason: Starts mid-sentence ('more. Everything from...') with garbled phrasing and no clean topic intro; ends mid-metaphor on 'just the tip of the dick of the iceberg' (unfinished) which also opens a NEW thread ('I'm testing all of them').
pairs.correct_reason: Opens on the claim 'On top of that, after sending everything for analytical testing, I think the research grade peptide market might be the dirtiest of them all', develops with the concrete Retatrutide test (claimed 60mg, was 108mg, 80% over), and RESOLVES at 'What that indicates is there's no quality control.' (42.2). Stops before the unfinished 'tip of the iceberg' metaphor and the new 'I'm testing all of them' thread.
pairs.hook: A lab test on a Retatrutide bottle showed 80% MORE than the label claimed, and that means zero quality control.

pairs.wrong_reason: Starts mid-sentence ('me, which is 50 micrograms...') referencing unclear prior context ('We are the same' at 311 refers backwards); ends by trailing into a NEW topic — 'I also should probably update you on the best growth hormone secretagogues... now I have a direct line to clinicians' (a tier-list segment).
pairs.correct_reason: Opens on the complete sentence 'Now, I've done it the way a clinician prescribed it to me, which is 50 micrograms three times a week right before bed, and it does work.', develops the tweaked hypertrophy dosing protocol (10mcg bilaterally pre-workout, intra-workout shake, 10mcg post-workout with meal, timing if on GH secretagogues), and RESOLVES at 'And it's awesome.' (341.6). Stops before the new tier-list topic.
pairs.hook: A clinician prescribed IGF-1 one way, but knowing the short half-life he TWEAKED the protocol for hypertrophy.

pairs.wrong_reason: Starts mid-sentence ('see is a lot of people cycle...'); ends mid-sentence on 'You don't want to you don't want to be in' — the sentence completes at 'that category' (182.2).
pairs.correct_reason: Opens on 'But what you will see is a lot of people cycle between taking a growth hormone secretagogue and IGF-1 because there's an inherent built-in long-loop negative feedback', develops the contrarian 'double fisting' take and the LR3-vs-FDA-version point, and RESOLVES on the complete sentence ending 'you have to be a broken kid to get that. You don't want to be in that category.' (182.2).
pairs.hook: Most people cycle growth hormone and IGF-1 separately, but he says double-fisting is the RIGHT way.

pairs.wrong_reason: Ends mid-thought as a NEW sub-point opens — 'most of it's beneficial action... some people benefit from systemic turmeric' bleeds into the turmeric-mechanism/gut-microbiome tangent that continues after the clip.
pairs.correct_reason: Opens on Huberman's complete anecdote 'I had reported my own anecdotal experience that taking turmeric really crushed my DHT levels and I did not feel good... it crushed all sorts of positive feelings of vitality', and RESOLVES at 'The moment I stopped taking turmeric, felt great again.' (8124.1). A complete personal-experience point; stops before Kyle pivots into turmeric's mechanism.
pairs.hook: Turmeric quietly CRUSHED his DHT and vitality, and he only felt right again after quitting it.
